          simp only [Factorization.mem_a, Params.initial, Multiset.mem_filter, Multiset.mem_join,
            Multiset.mem_replicate] at hm
          -- Extract the smoothness condition from the membership in the filtered list
          obtain ⟨⟨_, ⟨_, rfl⟩, hsmooth⟩, _⟩ := hm
          exact hsmooth
        -- Since m is smooth, its prime factors are ≤ L
        have h₄ : m.factorization p = 0 := by
          by_cases h₅ : p.Prime
          · -- If p is prime, then if p ∣ m, p ≤ L
            by_cases h₆ : p ∣ m
            · -- If p ∣ m, then p ≤ L because m is smooth
              have h₇ : p ≤ P.n / P.L := by
                have h₈ : p ∈ m.factors := by
                  rw [mem_factors] <;> aesop
                  <;> simp_all [h₅]

                have h₉ : p ∣ m := by
                  simpa [Nat.mem_factors] using h₈
                have h₁₀ : p ≤ P.n / P.L := by
                  have h₁₁ : m ∈ (P.n / P.L).smoothNumbers := h₃
                  have h₁₂ : p ≤ P.n / P.L := by
                    simp_all [Nat.mem_smoothNumbers]
                    <;> aesop
                  exact h₁₂
                exact h₁₀
              have h₁₁ : (p : ℕ) > Real.sqrt P.n := by
                have h₁₂ : (p : ℕ) > Real.sqrt P.n := by
                  have h₁₃ : (p : ℝ) ≤ Real.sqrt P.n := by
                    exact hp
                  have h₁₄ : Real.sqrt P.n < (p : ℝ) := by
                    -- This is a contradiction because p ≤ sqrt(n) and p > sqrt(n) cannot both be true
                    have h₁₅ : (p : ℝ) ≤ Real.sqrt P.n := by exact_mod_cast hp
                    have h₁₆ : Real.sqrt P.n < (p : ℝ) := by
                      -- This is a contradiction because p ≤ sqrt(n) and p > sqrt(n) cannot both be true
                      norm_num at h₁₅ ⊢ <;>
                      (try contradiction) <;>
                      (try nlinarith [Real.sqrt_nonneg (P.n : ℝ), Real.sq_sqrt (by positivity : 0 ≤ (P.n : ℝ))])
                    linarith
                  linarith
                linarith
              -- This leads to a contradiction because p ≤ sqrt(n) and p > sqrt(n) cannot both be true
              linarith
            · -- If p does not divide m, then the factorization of p in m is zero
              have h₇ : m.factorization p = 0 := by
                simp_all [Nat.factorization_eq_zero_iff]
                <;> aesop
              exact h₇
          · -- If p is not prime, then the factorization of p in m is zero
            have h₇ : m.factorization p = 0 := by
              simp_all [Nat.factorization_eq_zero_iff]
              <;> aesop
            exact h₇
        exact h₄
      -- The sum of the factorizations of p in the initial factors is zero
      have h₅ : (P.initial.a.map (·.factorization p)).sum = 0 := by
        calc
          (P.initial.a.map (·.factorization p)).sum = (P.initial.a.map (·.factorization p)).sum := rfl
